answer:
The work *San Hyan Mahajnana* (slug: `unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531`), a Medieval Kawi prose text, centers on the theme of supreme knowledge or wisdom (*mahājñāna*), explicitly titled in its opening and closing passages as "The Sacred Supreme Wisdom."[^1][^2]

This phrase is a distinctive trigram unique to the work (vs. the rest of the corpus) and frames the entire chapter, which describes states of consciousness (*jāgrat*, *svapna*, *suṣupti*, *tūrya*, etc.) associated with deities like Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra, and Maheśvara.[^3]

[^1]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch. 1, [p-573696](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-573696).
[^2]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch. 1, [p-0f7a95](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-0f7a95).
[^3]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch. 1 wiki card (key passages and trigrams).
